matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules also receive the actual sample REF -> ALT change; curated allele-change rules take precedence when the observed ALT allele or exact change has a known direction.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an mTORC1-S6K output-context thesis: any visible RPS6 locus signal should be treated as translational-control context because RPS6 is primarily interpreted through ribosomal protein S6 abundance and phosphorylation rather than through a mature common-variant phenotype catalog.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an mTORC1-S6K output-context thesis: any visible RPS6 locus signal should be treated as translational-control context because RPS6 is primarily interpreted through ribosomal protein S6 abundance and phosphorylation rather than through a mature common-variant phenotype catalog. An observed RPS6 variant places this sample in the curated RPS6 research context. The bundled RPS6 knowledge base is pathway oriented. RPS6 is typically interpreted as a translational-control and mTOR-output node rather than as a common-SNP clinical gene in routine VCF review. RPS6 is most commonly discussed as a signaling substrate and biomarker of pathway activity rather than as a locus with a broad common-variant literature.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: RPS6 phosphorylation is a canonical downstream readout of mTORC1-S6K activity in cancer and growth biology.
The gene is relevant to translational control, cell growth, proliferation, and treatment-response biomarker work.
Cancer biomarker studies using phospho-S6 as an mTOR-pathway activity readout.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a RPS6 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an mTORC1-S6K output-context thesis: any visible RPS6 locus signal should be treated as translational-control context because RPS6 is primarily interpreted through ribosomal protein S6 abundance and phosphorylation rather than through a mature common-variant phenotype catalog. RPS6 methylation should be read as local chromatin context around a compact ribosomal-protein locus. Because the biology of interest is often protein phosphorylation rather than gene methylation, these values are mainly supportive context. The bundled RPS6 knowledge base is pathway oriented. RPS6 is typically interpreted as a translational-control and mTOR-output node rather than as a common-SNP clinical gene in routine VCF review. RPS6 is most commonly discussed as a signaling substrate and biomarker of pathway activity rather than as a locus with a broad common-variant literature.
